title: Metrics Collection
description: Collecting quantitative measurements about system behavior: latency, error rates, throughput, resource utilization. Foundation for alerting and capacity planning.
best_practices: Use RED method (Rate, Errors, Duration) for services
Use USE method (Utilization, Saturation, Errors) for resources

title: Structured Logging
description: Logging in machine-parseable format (JSON) with consistent fields. Enables efficient searching, filtering, and analysis of log data at scale.

title: Distributed Tracing
description: Following requests across service boundaries to understand latency, dependencies, and error propagation in distributed systems.
best_practices: Correlate traces with logs and metrics

title: SLI, SLO, and SLA
description: Service Level Indicators (what to measure), Service Level Objectives (target values), and Service Level Agreements (contractual commitments) form the foundation of reliability management.
best_practices: Define SLIs based on user experience
Set achievable SLOs with error budgets
Use error budgets to balance reliability and velocity

title: Error Budgets
description: The acceptable amount of unreliability derived from SLO. If error budget is exhausted, focus shifts from features to reliability. Balances innovation with stability.
best_practices: Establish policies when budget is exhausted

title: Alerting Best Practices
description: Designing effective alerts that signal actionable problems without causing alert fatigue. Focus on symptoms over causes.
anti_patterns: Alert fatigue from too many alerts
Alerting on raw metrics without thresholds

title: Health Check Patterns
description: Endpoints that report service health status. Includes liveness (is the process running?), readiness (can it serve traffic?), and startup probes.
best_practices: Implement separate liveness and readiness checks
anti_patterns: Health checks that cause side effects

title: Dashboarding
description: Creating visual representations of system metrics and logs for real-time monitoring and historical analysis.
